member.office_address: 306 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0517

form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Appropriations Form for Office of Congressman Ro Khanna (CA-17)
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Below is the form for requesting support from Rep. Ro Khanna for programmatic or language federal appropriations requests. 

The deadline is March 11, 2026.

Take care to put the relevant information in the correct box. Summaries should be written as clearly and succinctly as possible. 

If you have more than one request, please submit this form once for each request. 

If this is a Community Project Funding request benefiting CA-17 community, please see our separate form for FY26 separate CPF submissions that will be launched in the future, pending guidance from the House Appropriations Committee.

sections.description: Details about the entity submitting the request and their primary point of contact.

fields.label: Name of Requesting Organization
Physical Address
City
State
Zip Code
Point of Contact Name
Point of Contact Title
Point of Contact Email
Point of Contact Anytime Phone
Nexus to the 17th District of CA (if none, write N/A)
fields.draft_value: NASA Science has invested over $21 million across CA-17 since FY2023, supporting high-tech employers like Aosense, Inc. in Sunnyvale ($8M for cold atom sensors) and research at San Jose State University.
fields.rationale: Cited aggregate spending ($21,280,573.97) and specific local recipients (Aosense, Inc. and SJSU) from the provided NASA spending context for CA-17.

sections.description: Details identifying the type of appropriation request and legislative categorization.

fields.label: Programmatic Funding or Legislative Language Request?
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding amount of $9 billion for the NASA Science Mission Directorate account.

fields.label: If Legislative Language Approps Request, is this for Bill or Report Language?
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, not a legislative language request.

fields.label: If Language Request, please include text of Language requested below
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this is a programmatic request with no bill or report language requested.

fields.label: Appropriations Bill / Subcommittee
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, and Science and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies
Interior, and Environment and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education and Related Agencies
Military Construction, and Veterans Affairs and Related Agencies
State, and Foreign Operations and Related Programs
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development  and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, and Science and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding is under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies subcommittee.

fields.label: Specific Agency and Office
fields.rationale: Standard reference for the requested agency and office.

fields.label: Appropriations Account
fields.rationale: The account name as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Program Title (if applicable)
fields.rationale: Matches the short title and program status in the reference values.

fields.label: Is this a New or an Existing Program?
fields.rationale: The NASA Science Mission Directorate is an established and existing program within NASA.

sections.description: Information regarding the requested amount and historical funding levels.

fields.label: Amount Requested for FY25 Appropriations (numbers only, no special characters like $ or ,)
fields.rationale: Based on the requested $9,000,000,000 in the reference funding ask.

fields.label: Amount Provided by FY2023 Consolidated Appropriations Act (P.L. 117-328)
fields.rationale: Used the pre-filled value of $7,250,000,000, formatted as numbers only per the likely intent of the form section, though the label specifies a specific Public Law usually associated with older years.

fields.label: Amount Provided by Relevant FY24 House Appropriations Bill (put unknown if you do not know)
fields.rationale: Utilized the pre-filled value which references the FY2026 enacted level as the most relevant recent baseline.

sections.description: Additional context, justifications, and coordination with other congressional offices.

fields.label: Please List Other Congressional Offices to Which This Request Is Also Being Made (if there are any in addition to Khanna)
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request. Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Are You Requesting That Rep. Khanna Lead a Multi-Member Letter in support?
fields.rationale: Reference values indicate that there is no request for the member to lead a multi-member letter.

fields.label: Is There an Existing Multi-Member Letter to Sign onto for This Request?
fields.rationale: This request is being submitted to multiple offices.

fields.label: If so, What is the Last Name of the Member Leading the Letter?
fields.rationale: The reference context indicates there is no multi-member letter lead at this time.

fields.label: Summary of Request (Provide Narrative Description No More than Four Sentences)
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion request for NASA Science Mission Directorate to support Decadal Survey priorities and U.S. space leadership
* Supports $21,280,573 in local NASA Science obligations within CA-17 across FY2023-FY2025
* Critical funding for Aosense, Inc. in Sunnyvale for cold atom gravity sensors and San Jose State University research
* Drives regional economic growth and maintains technical workforce stability after FY2026 budgetary disruptions
fields.rationale: Summarizes the $9B request, cites the $21.2M district spending total, and highlights local recipients Aosense, Inc. and SJSU from the spending context.

fields.label: Additional Background about the Request
fields.draft_value:
* Critical strategic investment to counter China's rapid expansion in lunar and planetary exploration
* Supports local innovation at Banpil Photonics (Santa Clara) and Leiden Measurement Technology (Sunnyvale)
* High ROI: every $1 invested in NASA returns $3 to the economy, boosting CA-17's tech sector
* Facilitates practical breakthroughs in climate modeling and planetary defense while training the next generation of STEM leaders
fields.rationale: Incorporates advocacy points on China and economic ROI while referencing local recipients Banpil Photonics and Leiden Measurement Technology.

fields.label: If you are recommending Rep. Khanna submit his own request rather than join another member-led letter, please send any suggested language to AppropsRequest.CA17@mail.house.gov as a Word attachment. (Note: You can use this template from the House Approps Committee for guidance: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1dwgAO3MQOPxjdliP_YSbaT2zxFuqpUUj1ihBq3DGyuM/edit?usp=sharing)
fields.help_text: If recommended, send suggested language as a Word attachment to AppropsRequest.CA17@mail.house.gov.
options: Not Yet but will do so
fields.rationale: The user is encouraged to sign onto the existing Bacon/Chu letter rather than initiating a separate individual request.

top_contracts.description: Igf::ot::igf SBIR phase iii award.  Phase i/ii awarded under nnx13cg02c cold atom gravity gradiometer for geodesy: the contractor shall design, construct, and optimize a high-performance gravity gradiometer based on light-pulse atom interferometry.  The contractor shall build and test a second prototype zero-dead-time (ZDT) absolute gravimeter.  The instruments in this phase iii effort takes advantage of technologies developed under NASA SBIR phase i and phase ii programs.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Building advanced gravity sensors using cold atom technology.

top_contracts.description: This is a firm-fixed price task order for the fy25 agency wide renewal of autodesk subscription software and services per attached quote # 25011846 dated 03/10/2025.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Annual renewal of essential engineering design and modeling software.

top_contracts.description: e014042 SBIR phase iii develop and demonstrate a broadband detector (0.20 to 50 micron) for measuring Earth'S radiation budget
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing a broadband detector for Earth's radiation budget measurement.

top_contracts.description: SBIR phase iii - life: luminescence imager for exploration
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing a luminescence imager for life detection in exploration.
Sustaining critical data processing and computational resources.

top_grants.recipient_name: San Jose State University Research Foundation
top_grants.description: 23-bpsf23-0050 examining climate impacts of cirrus clouds through past  present and future NASA airborne campaigns
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing high-altitude cloud impacts on Earth's climate systems

top_grants.recipient_name: San Jose State University Research Foundation
top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting broad science research for Moon, Mars, and space situational awareness.
